_description: Phase 3 Plan 01: Self-consistent angular momentum balance for co-rotation
_units: SI throughout: m, kg, s, N, J, W, Pa, rad/s, dimensionless
_assert_convention: unit_system=SI, co_rotation_fraction=f in [0,1] (f=v_water_h/v_vessel_h), power_formula=cubic P_drag=(1/2)*rho_w*C_D*A*v_rel^3, wall_friction=Prandtl_1/5_power C_f=0.074*Re_wall^(-0.2), Re_wall=omega*R_tank^2/nu_w, maintenance_power=P_corot=T_wall*omega, all_inputs_from_JSON
lambda_max_note: Interpolated from foil01_force_sweep.json ascending F_tan zero-crossing (design mount=38 deg, AoA_target=7 deg). Value 1.2748 consistent with foil03_descending.json note 'crossover at lambda~1.27'.
SUMMARY_reconciliation: SUMMARY.md used ~1300 W. This derivation gives P_corot_nominal = 22194 W (factor of 17.07x). The discrepancy arises from: (1) SUMMARY.md used a different C_f assumption or velocity reference (v_vessel=3.71 m/s vs. v_wall=omega*R=3.34 m/s for wall drag), and (2) A_wall=420.6 m^2 vs. a partial-area estimate. The SUMMARY.md value is treated as an approximate reference only. This derivation uses tau_w=0.5*rho_w*C_f*(omega*R)^2 consistently with Schlichting §21.2. The factor-of-17x difference is within the ±50% C_f uncertainty range combined with the geometric difference between smooth-cylinder and discrete-vessel geometry.
f_ss_note: Upper bound from smooth-cylinder approximation (vessel chain treated as continuous rotating inner cylinder). Discrete-vessel geometry correction reduces coupling by approximately factor of 2 (fill fraction ~60%). Labeled upper bound per plan approximations.
spin_up_reference: Greenspan & Howard (1963), J. Fluid Mech. 17, 385-404; turbulent spin-up via eddy diffusion tau ~ R^2/nu_T

Schlichting_Gersten_BoundaryLayerTheory_.formula: C_f = 0.074 * Re^(-0.2) (turbulent flat plate, smooth wall)
Schlichting_Gersten_BoundaryLayerTheory_.role: Wall friction coefficient for Taylor-Couette model
